skill.name: analyzing-component-quality
skill.description: Analyzes Claude Code components for marketplace quality, including descriptions, tool permissions, auto-invoke triggers, security posture, usability, and improvement priorities.
skill.summary: Scores Claude Code components and recommends practical quality improvements.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, weak-crypto, credential, and network patterns, but review found most are markdown examples or scoring text. No prompt injection, obfuscation, credential access, or network exfiltration was found. The remaining concern is that the skill grants Bash and ships a helper script that reads a caller-provided local path.

medium_findings.title: Bash Tool and Local Script Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash and documents running quality-scorer.py with a caller-provided path. The helper script reads local component files and prints a report, but Bash permission increases risk if future instructions use untrusted input unsafely.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Bash permission and helper script invocation are explicit. Semantic review found no write, network, or credential behavior, so the risk is execution capability rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and SAM Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography and Windows SAM matches occur in descriptive text, scoring labels, and output formatting. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, SAM database reads, password dumping, or credential collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible as normal prose or report formatting. Targeted review found no APIs or file paths associated with cryptography misuse or credential stores.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Are Not Runtime Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Many external-command detections are fenced markdown examples and inline tool lists. They explain how to assess components and do not execute by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious strings are inside markdown code fences or documentation tables. They are examples, not dynamic shell calls in executable code.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Does Not Indicate Network Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The only network indicator is a documentation link to Claude Code Plugin Documentation. No evidence found that the skill or Python script performs HTTP requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a markdown reference link. Review of the Python script found no requests, urllib, socket, curl, or similar network behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Permission Declared
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill frontmatter allows Bash, which enables local command execution when the assistant follows the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools field explicitly includes Bash. This is an execution capability even though no malicious command was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Supplied Path Read by Helper Script
dangerous_patterns.description: quality-scorer.py converts the first CLI argument to a Path and analyzes that file, which means users should provide only intended component files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code path is direct and visible. The behavior is local file reading, not exfiltration, so the severity remains moderate.

content.value_statement: Poorly described components are hard to invoke, secure, and maintain. This skill scores Claude Code components and suggests concrete improvements.
actual_capabilities: Scores descriptions, tool permissions, triggers, security, and usability on a 1 to 5 scale.
Reviews agents, skills, commands, and hooks using documented quality standards.
Identifies unclear descriptions, excessive tools, weak triggers, and documentation gaps.
Provides prioritized improvement suggestions with before-and-after guidance.
Includes a Python helper for heuristic scoring of markdown component files.
limitations: Assumes the component already passed technical validation.
Does not prove runtime safety or replace manual security review.
Some referenced helper files are described but not included in this package.
The scoring rubric is heuristic and may need human judgment for edge cases.

use_cases.title: Review a Skill Before Publishing
use_cases.description: Check whether a Claude Code skill has clear triggers, suitable tools, and useful documentation.

use_cases.title: Audit Agent Tool Permissions
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether an agent uses the smallest practical tool set for its stated purpose.

use_cases.title: Improve Internal Component Standards
use_cases.description: Apply a consistent scoring rubric across team-created skills, agents, commands, and hooks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one component file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Claude Code component for quality. Score each dimension and list the top three improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Tool Permission Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Review whether allowed tools match the component purpose.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the tool permissions for this component. Explain which tools are justified, risky, or unnecessary.

prompt_templates.title: Marketplace Readiness Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare a component for public distribution.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess whether this component is ready for a marketplace. Include quality scores, security concerns, and required changes.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Rubric Comparison
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare multiple components against the same standard.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these components using the quality rubric. Rank them, identify shared weaknesses, and propose standard improvements.

output_examples.input: A skill has a vague description and many broad tools.
output: Overall quality: adequate, with important improvements needed.
Primary issues: unclear triggers, excessive tool access, and missing examples.
Recommended action: narrow the tools and rewrite the description with specific activation phrases.

output_examples.input: An agent includes Task even though it performs a single focused review.
output: Tool permissions score decreases because delegation is not justified.
The recommendation is to remove Task or explain why orchestration is required.

output_examples.input: A marketplace author wants a final readiness check.
output: The report summarizes scores, strengths, required fixes, and projected quality after changes.
Security and usability findings are separated by priority.

best_practices: Review the component file and supporting references before assigning final scores.
Quote exact text when explaining weak descriptions, vague triggers, or risky permissions.
Prioritize fixes that reduce tool access, clarify invocation, or improve user outcomes.
anti_patterns: Do not treat technical validity as proof of marketplace quality.
Do not approve broad Bash, Write, Edit, or Task access without a clear need.
Do not give generic feedback when a specific rewrite or tool change is possible.

faq.question: What does this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: It evaluates component descriptions, tool permissions, triggers, security, usability, and improvement opportunities.

faq.question: Does it validate file structure?
faq.answer: No. It assumes structure and required fields were already validated by another process.

faq.question: Can it review Claude Code agents and skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers agents, skills, commands, and hooks with component-specific criteria.

faq.question: Does it include an automated scorer?
faq.answer: Yes. The included quality-scorer.py script provides heuristic scores for markdown component files.

faq.question: Is the scoring fully objective?
faq.answer: No. The rubric improves consistency, but reviewers should still apply judgment for context.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill grants Bash and can run a helper script that reads local files selected by the user.
